title: digitalis subalpina var. cedrorum
intro: Digitalis subalpina var. cedrorum, also known as Foxglove, is a plant species that belongs to the foxglove family (Plantaginaceae). It contains cardiac glycosides, particularly digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis subalpina var. cedrorum include improved cardiovascular health, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and using them in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although its use should be strictly monitored under medical supervision due to potential toxicity.
